findings.problem: The chapter lands Tommy's danger once at lines 115-117, then keeps translating that turn into exact dock logistics and a near-explicit drowning scenario. That over-proves the beat: instead of a comic chapter carrying an edge the reader feels but may not yet name, the close makes the lethal intention easy to name and weakens the later tonal gear change.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the first violent register shift at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:115; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:116; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:117, but revise the closing planning passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:237; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:239 so Tommy's thought stays on Cliff as a concrete threat and on Tommy's bodily capacity, not on a fully articulated submersion or fake-accident scenario. Strategy options: cut the explicit 'kept him there' logic, replace the accident-engineering sentences with one or two pressure-bearing physical observations, or end on Tommy choosing proximity rather than method.
findings.acceptance_test: After revising snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:231; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:237; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:239, a cold reader can tell Tommy is capable of violence and is moving toward a dangerous choice, but the passage does not explicitly state or paraphrase a murder method, holding Cliff underwater, or a fake-accident plan before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:245.

findings.problem: The chapter says Tommy is in the same chair he has used every September for twenty-five years, then in the same sentence says Martin bought the chairs the year after the 2002 fire. Those claims cannot both be true inside the established timeline: the specific Adirondacks are post-fire objects, not twenty-five-year constants.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:227 so the time claim matches the object's history. Either anchor the chair to the post-fire span, or separate the twenty-five-year continuity from the chair by making the repeated element the deck spot or ritual instead of this specific Adirondack.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:227, the passage yields only one coherent timeline: either the chair is explicitly post-fire in age, or the twenty-five-year continuity is attached to the place or habit instead of the chair. A reader should not be able to derive both claims from the same sentence.

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly falls into telegraphic fragment strings such as 'Craft beer. Leather loafers.' and 'The cabin. August 2002.' In this quantity they stop reading as selective pressure and start reading as a mechanical house tic. That weakens rhythm, clashes with the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration, and makes the chapter sound generated at the sentence level.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the fragment clusters at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:113; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:145; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:147; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:159; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:163; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:201; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235 into grammatically complete sentences that keep Tommy's blunt, muscular cadence without relying on stacked noun fragments. Keep the concrete nouns and hard stops, but rebuild the emphasis through syntax and sequencing rather than dropped verbs or article-stripped beats.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:113; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:145; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:147; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:159; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:163; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:233; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:235, the prose still reads terse and forceful, but a line-by-line read no longer encounters repeated stand-alone fragment chains of the same form.

summary: Tommy and Cliff's pressure scene is strong and the RT-document confrontation lands, but the chapter still fails review: the close over-telegraphs the lethal turn, line 227 contains a hard timeline contradiction about the Adirondack chairs, and the narration leans too often on telegraphic fragment strings that now read as a chapter-level tic rather than controlled style.
